title: [ci-skip] Github-Actions: Send e-mail on Cirrus cron failure
body:
This repository has a number of automaticly triggered branch-level
testing enabled.  However, other than remembering to go look at a
specific WebUI, there is no way for anybody to notice if/when these jobs
fail.

This commit introduces a github-action workflow which runs periodically,
checking for failed cron-triggered Cirrus-CI jobs.  When it finds any, it
formats a simple report for e-mail delivery.  The list of destination
addresses is configurable at any time by merging changes to a
simple CSV file.

Additional Work needed:

- [x] Gmail access setup for the robot account
- [x] Several secret env. vars. defined in the github repo. settings
- [x] Test the action using the containers/automation_sandbox repository

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.

> @cevich Any movement on this PR?

Yes, this automation can only work ***after*** it's already committed to the master branch (thanks GitHub).  So I've worked up a functional proof-of-concept using containers/automation_sandbox. For example, [successful detection of a failed Cirrus-CI cron job, and successful transmission of a notice e-mail](https://github.com/containers/automation_sandbox/runs/1365387430?check_suite_focus=true#step:6:25)

After solving all those problems, I'm hitting some common anti-spam smtp measures which require dealing with at the relay-level.  Otherwise nobody can receive the messages :confused:   This is the sendgrid.com (smtp relay) DNS validation request I brought up with @TomSweeneyRedHat 

All that said, I'd like to get the automation_sandbox POC fully working (including e-mail reception).  Then I'll port that effort over to this PR.  This compensates for the complete lack of ability to test the changes w/in a PR.

> Lots of new material for me here, it's going to take me a while to understand it all. 

Oh dang...I was intending to comment on this but didn't get a chance...

@vrothberg @edsantiago Please consider this at face-value (i.e. a draft), as there are some major external problems (sendgrind.com + spamhaus blocking) that may necessitate changes.  But I was hoping y'all could take a quick initial-pass over this as a sanity check?

I know Valentin has dabbled in github actions, and Ed's bash knowledge is legendary.  Would y'all mind focusing on these respective parts, so everyone can stay somewhat in their comfort zones?

@vrothberg @edsantiago oh, I should also mention that I developed this action over on `contianers/automation_sandbox`.  [Here's an example of it running and successfully sending out a fail-mail.](https://github.com/containers/automation_sandbox/runs/1413984745?check_suite_focus=true)

Due to the gawd-awful github security architecture...there is literally NO way to test this workflow until it hits the master branch :cry:

force-pushed updates suggested by @edsantiago.  [Test job that ran with those fixes.](https://github.com/containers/automation_sandbox/runs/1418848710?check_suite_focus=true) and the following is the e-mail that was sent:

```
Delivered-To: rh.container.bot@gmail.com
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=us-ascii
From: Do Not Reply <nobody@podman.io>
Subject: Cirrus-CI cron build failures on containers/automation_sandbox
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2020 15:09:49 +0000 (UTC)
To: cevich@redhat.com, rh.container.bot@gmail.com

This is your friendly neighborhood run2 github-action on containers/automat=
ion_sandbox.
I have detected that one or more Cirrus-CI cron-triggered jobs have failed =
recently:

Cron build 'FifteenMinutes' Failed: https://cirrus-ci.com/build/63186746319=
83104


This message was relayed through sendgrid.com by an automated system.  Repl=
ies to the sender will be ignored and/or discarded.  If you wish to be excl=
uded from receiving these messages in the future, please notify cevich@redh=
at.com or rh.container.bot@gmail.com

```

I dunno why it says `run2`...I was hoping it would say `check_cirrus_cron` :confounded:

LGTM
/approve
Waiting for @edsantiago approval and removal of the Draft flag.

Rebased/force-pushed.  More fixes for @edsantiago + updated e-mail format.  [Test job.](https://github.com/containers/automation_sandbox/runs/1419057223?check_suite_focus=true)  Delivered e-mail:

Force-pushed: Rebased + removed the unnecessary `$GITHUB_ENV` check from earlier (abandoned) implementation.

I arranged for an additional cron-job to fail, [this is shown in the most recent test job](https://github.com/containers/automation_sandbox/runs/1419836610?check_suite_focus=true) delivered message (below).

***Note:*** Until the `sendgrid.net` smtp relay fixes their listing on the spamhaus SBL, it will not be possible for successful delivery to any `redhat.com` addresses.  So initially, it will be up to the team to monitor the `rh.container.bot` (shared) email for notifications.  Once the spam-listing is fixed,  people are free to add/remove themselves from `contrib/cirrus/cron-fail_addrs.csv` list.

```

From: Do Not Reply <nobody@podman.io>

ies to the sender will be ignored and/or discarded.  If you wish to be excl=
uded from receiving these messages in the future, please notify cevich@redh=
at.com or rh.container.bot@gmail.com
```

@edsantiago @vrothberg I've updated everything based on your feedback and having manually tested it using `containers/automation_sandbox`.  IMHO, it's ready for final review when you have time.  Thanks.
Note: [I've added all the necessary secret variables to make this work.](https://github.com/containers/podman/settings/secrets/actions)
Minor fix: Removed unintended `cron-fail_addrs.csv` file from repository root.
